activities.title: Morning FNA Role Play — Agent & Client
materials: Week 1 persona or Week 2 validated segment
instructions: Pair with a squad mate and swap roles as advisor and client
8 min discovery · 4 min feedback · swap roles
Use Week 1 persona or Week 2 validated segment — listen, don't pitch
Capture what worked, what felt awkward, and what builds trust
debriefQuestions: What made the conversation feel natural?

activities.title: Role Play Reflection — Deck 01 Open
instructions: Coach presents Deck 01 Slide 1 — What did you learn this morning?
Pair-share: what surprised you, what was difficult, what felt natural
Connect morning experience to the FEC — you experienced client experience, you didn't just study it
outputs: Role play reflection notes in Playbook portfolio mission
debriefQuestions: What surprised you in the role play?
What made the conversation feel natural?

activities.title: Revise FEC Box 4 — Client Experience Blueprint
instructions: Coach presents Slides 2–3 — Great businesses are built from real experiences
Answer: What worked? What felt awkward? What builds trust?
Describe what your ideal client experience should look like from start to finish
Open Playbook → Edit FEC Box 4 — Client Experience Blueprint
debriefQuestions: What should your ideal client journey look like?

activities.title: Build FEC Box 5 — Winning Strategy
instructions: Coach presents Slide 4 — Compete on experience. Win with trust.
Answer: Why will clients choose your advisory practice instead of another?
Focus on experience, understanding, and trust — not products or price
Open Playbook → Edit FEC Box 5 — Winning Strategy
debriefQuestions: Why will clients choose your practice over another advisor?

activities.title: SPIKE Business Engine Canvas™ — Design Your Engine
instructions: Coach presents Slides 5–8 and 10–11 — The Business Engine and weekly operating system
Complete Page 1: Activity Engine, weekly targets, monthly projections (Weekly × 4)
Reflect: Can this engine produce the business you want?
Complete Page 2: Year 1 targets, business lever, growth simulation, revenue commitment
debriefQuestions: Can this engine produce the business you want?
Which business lever will you improve first?

activities.title: Squad Business Engine Share
instructions: Coach presents Slide 12 — Your venture now has an engine
Each squad presents its Business Engine in 3–5 minutes
Share: weekly engine numbers, chosen lever, and Year 1 revenue commitment
Close: Do you still have an idea — or do you now have a venture?
debriefQuestions: Do you still have an idea — or do you now have a venture?
What will you refine and repeat next week?
